content: so my 7 yr old daughter is autistic and we just had to meet with a dr for her disability review - he asked her to remember 3 words at the beginning - then he asked her to spell a couple word do some math and shape and colors then asked her the words -  she did well with shapes and colors and spelling but her math asked to substract 3 from 21 and said 19 and 3 from 19 and said 17 consistent 2  not 3 as asked - then was asked to repeat the 3 words and couldnt do it  - then the dr said we were done.

Reference reply from 13: <think>The user is sharing a personal experience about their 7-year-old autistic daughter and a disability review appointment with a doctor. It sounds like they might be worried about how her performance affected the outcome of the review.
They're describing what sounds like a cognitive assessment - the doctor was testing memory (3 words), attention/executive function (spelling, math), and recall (the 3 words again).
The user seems concerned or possibly upset. Let me be real with them:
